zh.lifeSummary: Margaret Boteler是Theobald Butler 4th Chief Butler of Ireland的女兒，出生。她於大約1315年與Walter De Trenoweth在英國英格蘭可瓦爾結婚。他們至少是3個兒子的父母。她去世於1344年在英國可瓦爾特雷格南。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Margaret Boteler是Theobald Butler 4th Chief Butler of Ireland的女儿，出生。她于大约1315年与Walter De Trenoweth在英国英格兰可瓦爾结婚。他们至少是3个儿子的父母。她去世于1344年在英国可瓦爾特雷格南。

nameMeanings.name: Boteler
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name for a maker of leather bottles, from an agent derivative of Middle English botel, Old French bouteille ‘bottle’.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: Margaret Boteler was born as the daughter of Theobald Butler 4th Chief Butler of Ireland. She married Walter De Trenoweth about 1315, in Cornwall, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons. She died in 1344, in Tregothnan, Cornwall, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Margaret Boteler est née, fille de Theobald Butler 4th Chief Butler of Ireland. Elle a épousé Walter De Trenoweth environ 1315, à Cornouailles, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils. Elle est décédée en 1344, à Tregothnan, Cornouailles, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Margaret Boteler wurde, als Tochter von Theobald Butler 4th Chief Butler of Ireland geboren. Sie hat um 1315 in Cornwall, England, United Kingdom Walter De Trenoweth geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne. Sie starb 1344 in Tregothnan, Cornwall, England.

it.lifeSummary: Margaret Boteler è nata il come figlia di Theobald Butler 4th Chief Butler of Ireland. Lei sposato Walter De Trenoweth circa 1315, nel Cornwall, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli. Morì nel 1344, nel Tregothnan, Cornwall, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Margaret BotelerはTheobald Butler 4th Chief Butler of Irelandの娘として生まれた。約1315年にイギリスイギリスコルンワルでWalter De Trenowethと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子の親であった。彼女は1344年にイギリスコルンワルトレゴスナンで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Boteler(은)는 Theobald Butler 4th Chief Butler of Ireland의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 대략 1315년 영국 잉글랜드 코른월에서 Walter De Trenoweth(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1344년 영국 코른월 트레고스난에서 사망했다.

mn.Truro, Cornwall, England: Truro, Kernev, Англи, Нэгдсэн Вант Улс

pt.lifeSummary: Margaret Boteler nasceu filha de Theobald Butler 4th Chief Butler of Ireland. Ela casou-se com Walter De Trenoweth aproximadamente 1315, em Cornwall, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos. Ela faleceu em 1344, em Tregothnan, Cornwall, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Margaret Boteler родилась. Её отца звали Theobald Butler 4th Chief Butler of Ireland. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Walter De Trenoweth около 1315 г. в местности корнвалл, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына. Она умерла в 1344 г. в местности треготнан, корнвалл, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Margaret Boteler nació como hija de Theobald Butler 4th Chief Butler of Ireland. Se casó con Walter De Trenoweth aproximadamente 1315, en Cornwall, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos. Murió en 1344, en Tregothnan, Cornwall, Inglaterra.
